skill.name: claude-skills
skill.description: Build and validate reusable Claude Skills from docs, APIs, code, and specifications. Includes templates, reference guidance, scaffolding, and a local quality gate for skill authors.
skill.summary: Create, refactor, scaffold, and validate reusable Claude Skills with clear triggers, boundaries, examples, and reference structure.
skill.author: 2025Emma

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are primarily Markdown code fences, template placeholders, local documentation links, and ordinary Bash helper commands. The only real security surface is local filesystem writes from the scaffold generator and local POSIX commands in the validator, which are expected for this meta-skill and do not show malicious intent or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Filesystem-Writing Scaffold Script
low_findings.description: The generator creates a target skill directory and writes SKILL.md plus reference files under a user-selected output directory. This is expected behavior for a scaffolding tool, but users should review the output path and use --force only intentionally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly performs local file creation, and the behavior matches its documented purpose. No evidence found of hidden deletion, persistence, credential access, or network transfer.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The validator and generator use standard local tools such as awk, grep, sed, mktemp, dirname, and pwd. The reviewed uses parse local Markdown/frontmatter and render templates rather than executing untrusted code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and arguments are visible and scoped to local files supplied by the user. No evidence found of shell eval, downloading code, background execution, or command construction from arbitrary script content.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to Claude, Anthropic, and GitHub documentation resources. They are reference links, not active network calls from scripts or skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are presented as documentation references and are not invoked by executable code. No evidence found of telemetry, webhooks, credential submission, or external command execution using these URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command and weak cryptography alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, inline backticks, template placeholders, headings, and examples. Review found no cryptographic implementation and no prompt-injection attempt in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are documentation examples or template text rather than executable risky code. A targeted search found no suspicious override, pre-approval, or instruction-skipping phrases.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need a repeatable way to turn source material into useful AI instructions. This meta-skill provides templates, structure, examples, and validation guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Scaffolds minimal or complete Skill directories with SKILL.md, references, scripts, and assets folders.
Defines activation triggers, boundaries, quick references, examples, and maintenance metadata for reusable Skills.
Provides templates for small domains and production-grade domains with references and quality gates.
Validates Skill frontmatter, required sections, reference structure, quick reference length, and example count.
Documents common authoring anti-patterns and practical fixes for unreliable or bloated Skills.
limitations: It does not provide domain expertise unless source material is supplied by the user.
The validator is heuristic and cannot prove that a Skill is correct or secure.
The scaffold script writes local files and should be run only in an intended output directory.
External documentation links may need manual verification if upstream Claude documentation changes.

use_cases.title: Create a New Domain Skill
use_cases.description: Turn product docs, API references, code samples, and support notes into a reusable Skill with clear triggers and examples.

use_cases.title: Refactor an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Split long SKILL.md content into references, sharpen activation rules, and add boundaries that prevent unwanted activation.

use_cases.title: Gate Skill Quality Before Release
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and validator to review structure, examples, references, and maintenance metadata before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the claude-skills guidance to create a minimal Skill for [domain]. Use these source materials: [paste links or excerpts]. Include triggers, boundaries, and three examples.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Documentation Into Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these docs and extract the most useful repeatable patterns for a Skill. Separate quick reference patterns from long reference material and identify missing facts.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md for activation reliability, boundaries, examples, and reference structure. Rewrite it so the Skill is concise, testable, and maintainable.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Release Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the claude-skills quality checklist to this Skill directory. Report failures, propose exact fixes, and distinguish evidence-backed claims from assumptions.

output_examples.input: A user provides API docs and asks for a new Skill.
output: A proposed Skill directory layout with SKILL.md, references, scripts, and assets as needed.
A concise frontmatter description with clear activation triggers.
Three reproducible examples with inputs, steps, and acceptance criteria.

output_examples.input: A maintainer provides a long SKILL.md that activates too often.
output: A refined set of use triggers and non-goals.
A shorter quick reference focused on directly usable patterns.
A reference split plan that moves long explanations out of the entrypoint.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks whether a Skill is ready for marketplace publication.
output: A checklist-based quality assessment with pass, warning, and fail items.
A focused remediation list for missing examples, vague descriptions, or unsupported claims.
A recommendation to validate locally with the included script when appropriate.

best_practices: Provide source material before asking the skill to write domain-specific guidance.
Keep SKILL.md focused on triggers, quick patterns, examples, boundaries, and maintenance notes.
Run the validator and review the checklist before publishing or sharing a Skill.
anti_patterns: Do not paste long documentation dumps into the quick reference section.
Do not claim unsupported API behavior without a verification path.
Do not run the scaffold script with --force unless overwriting the target is intentional.

faq.question: What does this Skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create reusable Claude Skills with SKILL.md, references, optional scripts, templates, examples, and validation guidance.

faq.question: Can it create a Skill without source material?
faq.answer: It can create a scaffold, but domain-specific guidance requires docs, APIs, code samples, or other evidence.

faq.question: Does the validator prove a Skill is safe?
faq.answer: No. It checks structure and common quality issues. Security and correctness still require human review.

faq.question: Does this Skill require network access?
faq.answer: No network access is required by the included scripts. The documentation includes external reference links for manual review.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The content is useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that author or maintain Skills.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing a generated Skill?
faq.answer: Review triggers, boundaries, examples, source citations, long reference files, and any local scripts included in the Skill.
